root_text.sn17.8:1.3: Assuttha no tumhe, bhikkhave, rattiyā paccūsasamayaṁ jarasiṅgālassa vassamānassā”ti?
root_text.sn17.8:1.5: “Eso kho, bhikkhave, jarasiṅgālo ukkaṇḍakena nāma rogajātena phuṭṭho neva bilagato ramati, na rukkhamūlagato ramati, na ajjhokāsagato ramati;
root_text.sn17.8:1.6: yena yena gacchati, yattha yattha tiṭṭhati, yattha yattha nisīdati, yattha yattha nipajjati; tattha tattha anayabyasanaṁ āpajjati.
root_text.sn17.8:1.7: Evameva kho, bhikkhave, idhekacco bhikkhu lābhasakkārasilokena abhibhūto pariyādiṇṇacitto neva suññāgāragato ramati, na rukkhamūlagato ramati, na ajjhokāsagato ramati;
root_text.sn17.8:1.8: yena yena gacchati, yattha yattha tiṭṭhati, yattha yattha nisīdati, yattha yattha nipajjati; tattha tattha anayabyasanaṁ āpajjati.

translation_text.sn17.8:1.3: Mendicants, did you hear an old jackal howling at the crack of dawn?”
translation_text.sn17.8:1.5: “That old jackal has the disease called mange. He’s not happy in his den, or at the root of a tree, or out in the open.
translation_text.sn17.8:1.6: Wherever he goes, stands, sits, or lies down he meets with tragedy and disaster.
translation_text.sn17.8:1.7: In the same way, take a certain mendicant whose mind is overcome and overwhelmed by possessions, honor, and popularity. They’re not happy in an empty hut, at the root of a tree, or out in the open.
translation_text.sn17.8:1.8: Wherever they go, stand, sit, or lie down they meet with tragedy and disaster.
translation_text.sn17.8:1.9: So grim are possessions, honor, and popularity. …”

variant_text.sn17.8:1.3: jarasiṅgālassa → jarasigālassa (bj, sya-all, km); siṅgālassa (pts1ed, pts2ed, mr)
variant_text.sn17.8:1.5: ukkaṇḍakena → ukkaṇṭakena (bj); ukkannakena (bj); ukkaṇṇakena (sya-all, km, pts1ed, pts2ed)
